input.description: Explore DMMF types and generation patterns
input.prompt:
I need to understand DMMF types and code generation patterns in the Prisma monorepo.

1. Find and read the DMMF type definitions — search for `DMMF` namespace, `Model`, `Field`, `Document` types. These might be in `packages/dmmf/`, `packages/generator-helper/`, or `packages/client/`. Read the actual type definitions.
2. Look at how existing generators transform DMMF models into TypeScript code. Check `packages/client-generator-js/src/` and `packages/client-generator-ts/src/` for patterns of iterating over models and generating types.
3. Look for any template/string generation utilities — `@prisma/ts-builders` package mentioned in AGENTS.md. Understand if there are helpers for generating TypeScript source code.
4. Check how generators write output files — look for `fs.writeFile` or similar patterns in existing generators, and how `options.generator.output` is used.
5. Look at the `packages/generator/` package if it exists — it may contain shared generator utilities.

Be thorough. I need to understand:
- The full DMMF type hierarchy (Document → datamodel → models → fields)
- How field types map to TypeScript types (String→string, Int→number, etc.)
- How generators write their output
- Any shared utilities for code generation

1	import path from 'node:path'
2	
3	import { BuildOptions } from './build'
4	
5	type AdapterEntry = {
6	  entry: string
7	  outfile: string
8	}
9	
10	export function createAdapterConfig(entries: AdapterEntry[]): BuildOptions[] {
11	  return entries.flatMap(({ entry, outfile }) => {
12	    const baseName = path.basename(outfile)
13	
14	    return [
15	      {
16	        name: `${baseName}-cjs`,
17	        format: 'cjs' as const,
18	        bundle: true,
19	        entryPoints: [entry],
20	        outfile,
21	        outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
22	        emitTypes: true,
23	      },
24	      {
25	        name: `${baseName}-esm`,
26	        format: 'esm' as const,
27	        bundle: true,
28	        entryPoints: [entry],
29	        outfile,
30	        outExtension: { '.js': '.mjs' },
31	        emitTypes: true,
32	      },
33	    ]
34	  })
35	}
36	
37	export const adapterConfig = createAdapterConfig([{ entry: 'src/index.ts', outfile: 'dist/index' }])
38	
39	export const unbundledConfig: BuildOptions[] = [
40	  {
41	    name: 'cjs',
42	    format: 'cjs',
43	    entryPoints: ['src/**/*.ts'],
44	    outdir: 'dist',
45	    outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
46	    emitTypes: true,
47	  },
48	  {
49	    name: 'esm',
50	    format: 'esm',
51	    entryPoints: ['src/**/*.ts'],
52	    outdir: 'dist',
53	    outExtension: { '.js': '.mjs' },
54	    emitTypes: true,
55	  },
56	]
57

1	name: CI Template job
2	
3	on:
4	  workflow_call:
5	    inputs:
6	      reason:
7	        description: |
8	          Reason why this workflow was called. Could be "buildpulse" for
9	          flaky tests detection or "daily-test" for scheduled full builds,
10	          "CI" for normal pushes/PRs
11	        type: string
12	        required: false
13	        default: CI
14	      jobTimeout:
15	        description: Timeout for the jobs. Default is 35 minutes
16	        type: number
17	        default: 35
18	        required: false
19	      jobsToRun:
20	        description: Jobs to run. See .github/workflows/scripts/detect-jobs-to-run.js script
21	        type: string
22	        default: '-all-'
23	        required: false
24	      pnpmVersion:
25	        description: pnpm package manager version
26	        type: string
27	        default: '10'
28	      engineHash:
29	        description: Customly-built engine hash to use
30	        type: string
31	        required: false
32	      driverAdapterTestJobTimeout:
33	        description: Timeout for driver adapter tests. Default is 60 minutes
34	        type: number
35	        default: 60
36	        required: false
37	    secrets:
38	      BUILDPULSE_ACCESS_KEY_ID:
39	        required: false
40	      BUILDPULSE_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY:
41	        required: false
42	      BOT_TOKEN:
43	        required: false
44	
45	env:
46	  HAS_BUILDPULSE_SECRETS: ${{ secrets.BUILDPULSE_ACCESS_KEY_ID != '' && secrets.BUILDPULSE_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY != '' }}
47	  PRISMA_TELEMETRY_INFORMATION: 'prisma test.yml'
48	  # To hide "Update available 0.0.0 -> x.x.x"
49	  PRISMA_HIDE_UPDATE_MESSAGE: true
50	  FUNCTIONAL_TEST_OPTIONS: --detectOpenHandles
51	
52	concurrency:
53	  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}
54	  # Don't cancel if called from buildpulse workflow
55	  cancel-in-progress: ${{ inputs.reason != 'buildpulse' }}
56	
57	jobs:
58	  #
59	  # Linting
60	  #
61	  lint:
62	    name: Lint
63	    timeout-minutes: ${{ inputs.jobTimeout }}
64	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
65	
66	    steps:
67	      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
68	
69	      - name: Install & build
70	        uses: ./.github/actions/setup
71	        with:
72	          node-version: '20.19.0'
73	          engine-hash: ${{ inputs.engineHash }}
74	          skip-tsc: false
75	
76	      # https://github.com/actions/toolkit/blob/master/docs/commands.md#problem-matchers
77	      # Matchers are added in setup-node
78	      # https://github.com/actions/setup-node/blob/bacd6b4b3ac3127b28a1e1920c23bf1c2cadbb85/src/main.ts#L54-L60
79	      - name: Disable GitHub annotations from linting
80	        run: |
81	          echo "::remove-matcher owner=eslint-compact::"
82	          echo "::remove-matcher owner=eslint-stylish::"
83	
84	      - name: Run eslint
85	        run: pnpm run lint
86	        working-directory: ./
87	
88	      - name: Run prettier-check
89	        run: pnpm run prettier-check
90	        working-directory: ./
91	
92	      - name: Run check-engines-override
93	        run: pnpm run check-engines-override
94	        working-directory: ./
95	
96	      - name: Run pnpm audit (production dependencies only)
97	        run: pnpm audit --prod
98	
99	  #
100	  # CLIENT (legacy tests without types test)

5	export interface GeneratorConfig {

18	    previewFeatures?: never

39	export type ConnectorType =
40	  | 'mysql'
41	  | 'mongodb'

159	    name: Client Generator Prisma Client JS [v${{ matrix.node }}] ${{ matrix.shard }}
160	    timeout-minutes: ${{ inputs.driverAdapterTestJobTimeout }}
161	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
162	    if: |
163	      contains(inputs.jobsToRun, '-all-') ||
164	      contains(inputs.jobsToRun, '-client-')
165	    strategy:
166	      fail-fast: false
167	      matrix:
168	        shard: ['1/6', '2/6', '3/6', '4/6', '5/6', '6/6']
169	        node: ['20.19.0', '22.12.0', '24']
170	        flavor: ['js_pg']
171	    steps:
172	      - uses: actions/checkout@v6
173	
174	      - name: Start Docker Compose Services
175	        uses: nick-fields/retry@v3
176	        with:
177	          timeout_minutes: 10
178	          max_attempts: 5
179	          retry_wait_seconds: 120
180	          command: docker compose -f docker/docker-compose.yml up --wait --detach postgres
181	
182	      - name: Install & build
183	        uses: ./.github/actions/setup
184	        with:
185	          node-version: ${{ matrix.node }}
186	          engine-hash: ${{ inputs.engineHash }}
187	          skip-tsc: false
188	
189	      - run: pnpm run test:functional ${{ env.FUNCTIONAL_TEST_OPTIONS }} --adapter ${{ matrix.flavor }} --shard ${{ matrix.shard }} --generator-type prisma-client-js --client-runtime client --runInBand
190	        working-directory: packages/client
191	        env:
192	          NODE_NO_WARNINGS: 1 # hides undici websocket warnings
193	          NODE_OPTIONS: '--max-old-space-size=8096'
194	          JEST_JUNIT_SUITE_NAME: 'client/functional'
195	          JEST_JUNIT_UNIQUE_OUTPUT_NAME: true
196	
197	      - name: Upload test results to BuildPulse for flaky test detection
198	        # Only run this step for branches where we have access to secrets.
199	        # Run this step even when the tests fail. Skip if the workflow is cancelled.
200	        if: env.HAS_BUILDPULSE_SECRETS == 'true' && !cancelled() && inputs.reason == 'buildpulse' && github.repository == 'prisma/prisma'
201	        uses: buildpulse/buildpulse-action@v0.11.0
202	        with:
203	          account: 17219288
204	          repository: 192925833
205	          path: packages/*/junit*.xml
206	          key: ${{ secrets.BUILDPULSE_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}
207	          secret: ${{ secrets.BUILDPULSE_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}
208	
209	  #
210	  # CLIENT (functional tests with driver adapters)
211	  #
212	  client-driveradapters:
213	    name: DA ${{ matrix.flavor }} ${{ matrix.clientRuntime }} ${{ matrix.previewFeatures }} [v${{ matrix.node }}] ${{ matrix.shard }}
214	    timeout-minutes: ${{ inputs.driverAdapterTestJobTimeout }}
215	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
216	    if: |
217	      contains(inputs.jobsToRun, '-all-') ||
218	      contains(inputs.jobsToRun, '-client-')
219	    strategy:
220	      fail-fast: false
221	      # Note: the maximum number of matrix configurations on Github Actions is 256
222	      matrix:
223	        clientRuntime: ['client']
224	        flavor:
225	          [
226	            'js_pg',
227	            'js_neon',
228	            'js_libsql',
229	            'js_planetscale',
230	            'js_d1',
231	            'js_better_sqlite3',
232	            'js_mssql',
233	            'js_mariadb',
234	            'js_pg_cockroachdb',
235	          ]
236	        shard: ['1/4', '2/4', '3/4', '4/4']
237	        node: ['20.19.0']

5	export interface GeneratorConfig {
6	  name: string
7	  output: EnvValue | null
8	  isCustomOutput?: boolean
9	  provider: EnvValue
10	  config: {
11	    /** `output` is a reserved name and will only be available directly at `generator.output` */
12	    output?: never
13	    /** `provider` is a reserved name and will only be available directly at `generator.provider` */
14	    provider?: never
15	    /** `binaryTargets` is a reserved name and will only be available directly at `generator.binaryTargets` */
16	    binaryTargets?: never
17	    /** `previewFeatures` is a reserved name and will only be available directly at `generator.previewFeatures` */
18	    previewFeatures?: never
19	  } & {
20	    [key: string]: string | string[] | undefined
21	  }
22	  binaryTargets: BinaryTargetsEnvValue[]
23	  // TODO why is this not optional?
24	  previewFeatures: string[]
25	  sourceFilePath: string
26	}
27	
28	export interface EnvValue {
29	  fromEnvVar: null | string
30	  value: null | string
31	}
32	
33	export interface BinaryTargetsEnvValue {
34	  fromEnvVar: string | null
35	  value: string
36	  native?: boolean
37	}
38	
39	export type ConnectorType =
40	  | 'mysql'
41	  | 'mongodb'
42	  | 'sqlite'
43	  | 'postgresql'
44	  | 'postgres' // TODO: we could normalize postgres to postgresql this in engines to reduce the complexity?
45	  | 'prisma+postgres' // Note: used for Prisma Postgres, managed by PDP
46	  | 'sqlserver'
47	  | 'cockroachdb'
48	
49	export type ActiveConnectorType = Exclude<ConnectorType, 'postgres' | 'prisma+postgres'>
50	
51	export interface DataSource {
52	  name: string
53	  provider: ConnectorType
54	  // In Rust, this comes from `Connector::provider_name()`
55	  activeProvider: ActiveConnectorType
56	  schemas: string[] | []
57	  sourceFilePath: string
58	}
59	
60	export type BinaryPaths = {
61	  schemaEngine?: { [binaryTarget: string]: string } // key: target, value: path
62	}
63	
64	/** The options passed to the generator implementations */
65	export type GeneratorOptions = {
66	  generator: GeneratorConfig
67	  // TODO: what is otherGenerators for?
68	  otherGenerators: GeneratorConfig[]
69	  schemaPath: string
70	  dmmf: DMMF.Document
71	  datasources: DataSource[]
72	  // TODO deprecate datamodel & rename to schema?
73	  datamodel: string
74	  // TODO is it really always version hash? Feature is unclear.
75	  version: string // version hash
76	  binaryPaths?: BinaryPaths
77	  noHints?: boolean
78	  allowNoModels?: boolean
79	  typedSql?: SqlQueryOutput[]
80	}
81	
82	export type EngineType = 'schemaEngine'
83	
84	export type GeneratorManifest = {
85	  prettyName?: string
86	  defaultOutput?: string
87	  denylists?: {
88	    models?: string[]
89	    fields?: string[]
90	  }
91	  requiresGenerators?: string[]
92	  requiresEngines?: EngineType[]
93	  version?: string
94	  requiresEngineVersion?: string
95	}

34	    },
35	    datamodel: '',

1	import { enginesVersion } from '@prisma/engines-version'
2	import { Generator, GeneratorConfig, GeneratorManifest, GeneratorOptions } from '@prisma/generator'
3	import { parseEnvValue } from '@prisma/internals'
4	import { getTsconfig } from 'get-tsconfig'
5	import { bold, dim, green } from 'kleur/colors'
6	
7	import { version as clientVersion } from '../package.json'
8	import { inferImportFileExtension, parseGeneratedFileExtension, parseImportFileExtension } from './file-extensions'
9	import { generateClient } from './generateClient'
10	import { inferModuleFormat, parseModuleFormatFromUnknown } from './module-format'
11	import { parseRuntimeTargetFromUnknown, RuntimeTargetInternal } from './runtime-targets'
12	
13	const missingOutputErrorMessage = `An output path is required for the \`prisma-client\` generator. Please provide an output path in your schema file:
14	
15	${dim(`generator client {
16	  provider = "prisma-client"`)}
17	${green('  output   = "../src/generated"')}
18	${dim('}')}
19	
20	${bold('Note:')} the output path is relative to the schema directory.
21	`
22	
23	function getOutputPath(config: GeneratorConfig): string {
24	  if (!config.output) {
25	    throw new Error(missingOutputErrorMessage)
26	  }
27	  return parseEnvValue(config.output)
28	}

30	export class PrismaClientTsGenerator implements Generator {
31	  readonly name = 'prisma-client-ts'
32	
33	  getManifest(config: GeneratorConfig): Promise<GeneratorManifest> {
34	    return Promise.resolve({
35	      defaultOutput: getOutputPath(config),
36	      prettyName: 'Prisma Client',
37	      version: clientVersion,
38	      requiresEngines: [],
39	      requiresEngineVersion: enginesVersion,
40	    })
41	  }
42	
43	  async generate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
44	    const { config } = options.generator
45	    const outputDir = getOutputPath(options.generator)
46	    const tsconfig = getTsconfig(outputDir)?.config
47	
48	    const target = config.runtime !== undefined ? parseRuntimeTargetFromUnknown(config.runtime) : 'nodejs'
49	
50	    const generatedFileExtension =
51	      config.generatedFileExtension !== undefined ? parseGeneratedFileExtension(config.generatedFileExtension) : 'ts'
52	
53	    const importFileExtension =
54	      config.importFileExtension !== undefined
55	        ? parseImportFileExtension(config.importFileExtension)
56	        : inferImportFileExtension({
57	            tsconfig,
58	            generatedFileExtension,
59	            target,
60	          })
61	
62	    const moduleFormat =
63	      config.moduleFormat !== undefined
64	        ? parseModuleFormatFromUnknown(config.moduleFormat)
65	        : inferModuleFormat({
66	            tsconfig,
67	            generatedFileExtension,
68	            importFileExtension,
69	          })
70	
71	    await generateClient({
72	      datamodel: options.datamodel,
73	      schemaPath: options.schemaPath,
74	      binaryPaths: options.binaryPaths!,
75	      datasources: options.datasources,
76	      outputDir,
77	      runtimeBase: '@prisma/client/runtime',
78	      dmmf: options.dmmf,
79	      generator: options.generator,
80	      engineVersion: options.version,
81	      clientVersion,
82	      activeProvider: options.datasources[0]?.activeProvider,
83	      typedSql: options.typedSql,
84	      target,
85	      generatedFileExtension,
86	      importFileExtension,
87	      moduleFormat,
88	      tsNoCheckPreamble: true, // Set to false only during internal tests
89	      compilerBuild: parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(options.generator.config.compilerBuild, target),
90	    })
91	  }
92	}
93	
94	function parseCompilerBuildFromUnknown(value: unknown, target: RuntimeTargetInternal): 'fast' | 'small' {
95	  if (value === undefined) {
96	    // using the 'small' build for 'vercel-edge' target to fit within their free tier limits
97	    return target === 'vercel-edge' ? 'small' : 'fast'
98	  }
99	  if (value === 'small' || value === 'fast') {
100	    return value
101	  }
102	  throw new Error(`Invalid compiler build: ${JSON.stringify(value)}, expected one of: "fast", "small"`)
103	}

56	function getExecutable(name: string): string {
57	  let fullName = path.join(__dirname, name)
58	  if (process.platform === 'win32') {
59	    fullName += '.cmd'
60	  }
61	  return fullName
62	}
63	
64	describe('generatorHandler', () => {
65	  // TODO: Windows: this test fails with ENOENT on CI because it can't file the .cmd file:
66	  //   spawn D:\\a\\prisma\\prisma\\packages\\generator-helper\\src\\__tests__\\exiting-executable.cmd ENOENT
67	  // This does not happen on Windows locally.
68	  testIf(process.platform !== 'win32')('exiting', async () => {
69	    const generator = new GeneratorProcess(getExecutable('exiting-executable'))
70	    await generator.init()
71	    try {
72	      await generator.generate(stubOptions)
73	      generator.stop()
74	    } catch (e) {
75	      expect(e.message).toContain('Console error before exit')
76	    }
77	  })
78	
79	  // TODO: Windows: this test fails with ENOENT on CI because it can't file the .cmd file:
80	  //   spawn D:\\a\\prisma\\prisma\\packages\\generator-helper\\src\\__tests__\\invalid-executable.cmd ENOENT
81	  // This does not happen on Windows locally.
82	  testIf(process.platform !== 'win32')(
83	    'parsing error',
84	    async () => {
85	      const generator = new GeneratorProcess(getExecutable('invalid-executable'))
86	      await generator.init()
87	      await expect(() => generator.getManifest(stubOptions.generator)).rejects.toThrow('Cannot find module')
88	    },
89	    10_000,
90	  )
91	
92	  test('minimal-executable', async () => {
93	    const generator = new GeneratorProcess(getExecutable('minimal-executable'))
94	    await generator.init()
95	    const manifest = await generator.getManifest(stubOptions.generator)
96	    expect(manifest).toMatchInlineSnapshot(`
97	      {
98	        "defaultOutput": "default-output",
99	        "denylists": {
100	          "models": [
101	            "SomeForbiddenModel",
102	          ],
103	        },
104	        "prettyName": "This is a pretty name",
105	        "requiresEngines": [
106	          "schema-engine",
107	        ],
108	        "requiresGenerators": [
109	          "prisma-client-js",
110	        ],
111	      }
112	    `)
113	    expect(() => generator.generate(stubOptions)).not.toThrow()
114	
115	    generator.stop()
116	  })
117	
118	  test('failing-executable', async () => {
119	    const generator = new GeneratorProcess(getExecutable('failing-executable'))
120	    await generator.init()
121	    await expect(generator.getManifest(stubOptions.generator)).rejects.toThrow()
122	    await expect(generator.generate(stubOptions)).rejects.toThrow()
123	    generator.stop()
124	  })

126	  test('failing-after-1s-executable', async () => {
127	    const generator = new GeneratorProcess(getExecutable('failing-after-1s-executable'))
128	    await generator.init()
129	    await expect(generator.getManifest(stubOptions.generator)).rejects.toThrow('test')
130	    generator.stop()
131	  })

1	import { ChokidarOptions, watch as createWatcher } from 'chokidar'
2	import * as esbuild from 'esbuild'
3	import { BuildContext } from 'esbuild'
4	import { writeFileSync } from 'fs'
5	import glob from 'globby'
6	import path from 'path'
7	
8	import { debounce } from '../blaze/debounce'
9	import { flatten } from '../blaze/flatten'
10	import { handle } from '../blaze/handle'
11	import { map } from '../blaze/map'
12	import { omit } from '../blaze/omit'
13	import { pipe } from '../blaze/pipe'
14	import { transduce } from '../blaze/transduce'
15	import { fixImportsPlugin } from './plugins/fixImportsPlugin'
16	import { onErrorPlugin } from './plugins/onErrorPlugin'
17	import { resolvePathsPlugin } from './plugins/resolvePathsPlugin'
18	import { tscPlugin } from './plugins/tscPlugin'
19	
20	export type BuildResult = esbuild.BuildResult
21	export type BuildOptions = esbuild.BuildOptions & {
22	  name?: string
23	  emitTypes?: boolean
24	  emitMetafile?: boolean
25	  outbase?: never // we don't support this
26	}
27	
28	const DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS = {
29	  platform: 'node',
30	  target: 'ES2022',
31	  logLevel: 'error',
32	  tsconfig: 'tsconfig.build.json',
33	  metafile: true,
34	} as const
35	
36	/**
37	 * Apply defaults to the original build options
38	 * @param options the original build options
39	 */
40	const applyDefaults = (options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions => ({
41	  ...DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS,
42	  format: 'cjs',
43	  outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
44	  resolveExtensions: ['.ts', '.js', '.node'],
45	  entryPoints: glob.sync('./src/**/*.{j,t}s', {
46	    ignore: ['./src/__tests__/**/*'],
47	  }),
48	  mainFields: ['module', 'main'],
49	  ...options,
50	  // outfile has precedence over outdir, hence these ternaries
51	  outfile: options.outfile ? getOutFile(options) : undefined,
52	  outdir: options.outfile ? undefined : getOutDir(options),
53	  plugins: [
54	    ...(options.plugins ?? []),
55	    resolvePathsPlugin,
56	    fixImportsPlugin,
57	    tscPlugin(options.emitTypes),
58	    onErrorPlugin,
59	  ],
60	  external: [...(options.external ?? []), ...getProjectExternals(options)],
61	})
62	
63	/**
64	 * Create two deferred builds for esm and cjs. The one follows the other:
65	 * - 1. The code gets compiled to an optimized tree-shaken esm output
66	 * - 2. We take that output and compile it to an optimized cjs output
67	 * @param options the original build options
68	 * @returns if options = [a, b], we get [a-esm, a-cjs, b-esm, b-cjs]
69	 */
70	function createBuildOptions(options: BuildOptions[]) {
71	  return flatten(
72	    map(options, (options) => [
73	      // we defer it so that we don't trigger glob immediately
74	      () => applyDefaults(options),
75	      // ... here can go more steps
76	    ]),
77	  )
78	}
79	
80	/**
81	 * We only want to trigger the glob search once we are ready, and that is when
82	 * the previous build has finished. We get the build options from the deferred.
83	 */
84	function computeOptions(options: () => BuildOptions) {
85	  return options()
86	}
87	
88	/**
89	 * Extensions are not automatically by esbuild set for `options.outfile`. We
90	 * look at the set `options.outExtension` and we add that to `options.outfile`.
91	 */
92	function addExtensionFormat(options: BuildOptions) {
93	  if (options.outfile && options.outExtension) {
94	    const ext = options.outExtension['.js']
95	
96	    options.outfile = `${options.outfile}${ext}`
97	  }
98	
99	  return options
100	}
101	
102	/**
103	 * If we don't have `options.outfile`, we default `options.outdir`
104	 */
105	function addDefaultOutDir(options: BuildOptions) {
106	  if (options.outfile === undefined) {
107	    options.outdir = getOutDir(options)
108	  }
109	
110	  return options
111	}
112	
113	/**
114	 * Execute esbuild with all the configurations we pass
115	 */
116	async function executeEsBuild(options: BuildOptions) {
117	  if (process.env.WATCH === 'true') {
118	    const context = await esbuild.context(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
119	
120	    watch(context, options)
121	  }
122	
123	  const build = await esbuild.build(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
124	  const outdir = options.outdir ?? (options.outfile ? path.dirname(options.outfile) : undefined)
125	
126	  if (build.metafile && options.emitMetafile) {
127	    const metafilePath = `${outdir}/${options.name}.meta.json`
128	    writeFileSync(metafilePath, JSON.stringify(build.metafile))
129	  }
130	
131	  return [options, build] as const
132	}
133	
134	/**
135	 * Execution pipeline that applies a set of actions
136	 * @param options
137	 */
138	export async function build(options: BuildOptions[]) {
139	  return transduce.async(
140	    createBuildOptions(options),
141	    pipe.async(computeOptions, logStartBuild, addExtensionFormat, addDefaultOutDir, executeEsBuild),
142	  )
143	}
144	
145	/**
146	 * Prints a message every time a new bundle is built
147	 */
148	function logStartBuild(options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions {
149	  console.log(`Building ${options.name} as ${options.format ?? 'cjs'}...`)
150	  return options
151	}
152	
153	/**
154	 * Executes the build and rebuilds what is necessary
155	 * @param builds
156	 */
157	const watch = (context: BuildContext, options: BuildOptions) => {
158	  if (process.env.WATCH !== 'true') return context
159	
160	  // common chokidar options for the watchers
161	  const config = {
162	    ignoreInitial: true,
163	    ignored: [/$src\/__tests__\//, 'package.json'],
164	  } satisfies ChokidarOptions
165	
166	  // prepare the incremental builds watcher
167	  const changeWatcher = createWatcher(['./src'], config)
168	
169	  // triggers quick rebuild on file change
170	  const fastRebuild = debounce(async () => {
171	    const timeBefore = Date.now()
172	
173	    // we handle possible rebuild exceptions
174	    const rebuildResult = await handle.async(() => {
175	      return context.rebuild()
176	    })
177	
178	    // Handle unexpected internal errors
179	    if (rebuildResult instanceof Error) {
180	      console.error(rebuildResult)
181	      // Handle build errors (e.g., syntax errors)
182	    } else if (rebuildResult.errors.length > 0) {
183	      // Log the detailed error object from esbuild for better debugging.
184	      console.error(rebuildResult.errors)
185	    }
186	
187	    console.log(`${Date.now() - timeBefore}ms [${options.name ?? ''}]`)
188	  }, 10)
189	
190	  changeWatcher.on('change', fastRebuild)
191	
192	  return undefined
193	}
194	
195	// Utils ::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
196	
197	// get a default directory if needed (no outfile)
198	function getOutDir(options: BuildOptions) {
199	  if (options.outfile !== undefined) {
200	    return path.dirname(options.outfile)
201	  }
202	
203	  return options.outdir ?? 'dist'
204	}
205	
206	// get the output file from an original path
207	function getOutFile(options: BuildOptions) {
208	  if (options.outfile !== undefined) {
209	    const dirname = getOutDir(options)
210	    const filename = path.basename(options.outfile)
211	
212	    return `${dirname}/${filename}`
213	  }
214	
215	  return undefined
216	}
217	
218	// get the current project externals this helps to mark dependencies as external
219	// by having convention in the package.json (dev = bundled, non-dev = external)
220	function getProjectExternals(options: BuildOptions) {
221	  const pkg = require(`${process.cwd()}/package.json`)
222	  const peerDeps = Object.keys(pkg.peerDependencies ?? {})
223	  const regDeps = Object.keys(pkg.dependencies ?? {})
224	
225	  // when bundling, only the devDeps will be bundled
226	  if (!process.env.IGNORE_EXTERNALS && options.bundle === true) {
227	    return [...new Set([...peerDeps, ...regDeps])]
228	  }
229	
230	  // otherwise, all the dependencies will be bundled
231	  return []
232	}

5	export function modelExports(context: GenerateContext): string[] {
6	  return (
7	    Object.values(context.dmmf.typeAndModelMap)
8	      // choose models that have output types
9	      .filter((model) => context.dmmf.outputTypeMap.model[model.name])
10	
11	      // generate export statements
12	      .map((model) => {
13	        const docLines = model.documentation ?? ''
14	        const modelLine = `Model ${model.name}\n`
15	        const docs = `${modelLine}${docLines}`
16	        const suffix = 'Model'
17	
18	        const modelTypeExport = ts
19	          // e.g., `export type User = Prisma.UserModel` when `model.name` is `User`
20	          .moduleExport(ts.typeDeclaration(model.name, ts.namedType(`Prisma.${model.name}${suffix}`)))
21	          // add JSDoc comment with the model name and documentation
22	          .setDocComment(ts.docComment(docs))
23	
24	        return ts.stringify(modelTypeExport)
25	      })
26	  )
27	}

28	  const method = ts
29	    .method('$transaction')
30	    .setDocComment(
31	      ts.docComment`
32	        Allows the running of a sequence of read/write operations that are guaranteed to either succeed or fail as a whole.
33	        @example
34	        \`\`\`
35	        const [george, bob, alice] = await prisma.$transaction([
36	          prisma.user.create({ data: { name: 'George' } }),
37	          prisma.user.create({ data: { name: 'Bob' } }),
38	          prisma.user.create({ data: { name: 'Alice' } }),
39	        ])
40	        \`\`\`
41	
42	        Read more in our [docs](https://www.prisma.io/docs/orm/prisma-client/queries/transactions).

44	    )
45	    .addGenericParameter(ts.genericParameter('P').extends(ts.array(tsx.prismaPromise(ts.anyType))))
46	    .addParameter(ts.parameter('arg', ts.arraySpread(ts.namedType('P'))))
47	    .setReturnType(tsx.promise(ts.namedType('runtime.Types.Utils.UnwrapTuple').addGenericArgument(ts.namedType('P'))))
48	
49	  if (context.dmmf.hasEnumInNamespace('TransactionIsolationLevel', 'prisma')) {
50	    const options = ts
51	      .objectType()
52	      .formatInline()
53	      .add(ts.property('isolationLevel', ts.namedType('Prisma.TransactionIsolationLevel')).optional())
54	    method.addParameter(ts.parameter('options', options).optional())
55	  }
56	
57	  return ts.stringify(method, { indentLevel: 1, newLine: 'leading' })
58	}

2	import * as DMMF from '@prisma/dmmf'
3	import pluralize from 'pluralize'
4	
5	export function getCountAggregateOutputName(modelName: string): string {

9	/**
10	 * Turns type: string into type: string[] for all args in order to support union input types
11	 * @param document
12	 */
13	export function externalToInternalDmmf(document: DMMF.Document): DMMF.Document {
14	  return {
15	    ...document,
16	    mappings: getMappings(document.mappings, document.datamodel),
17	  }
18	}

20	function getMappings(mappings: DMMF.Mappings, datamodel: DMMF.Datamodel): DMMF.Mappings {
21	  const modelOperations = mappings.modelOperations
22	    .filter((mapping) => {
23	      const model = datamodel.models.find((m) => m.name === mapping.model)
24	      if (!model) {
25	        throw new Error(`Mapping without model ${mapping.model}`)
26	      }
27	      return model.fields.some((f) => f.kind !== 'object')
28	    })
29	    // TODO most of this is probably not needed anymore
30	    .map((mapping: any) => ({
31	      model: mapping.model,
32	      plural: pluralize(uncapitalize(mapping.model)), // TODO not needed anymore
33	      findUnique: mapping.findUnique || mapping.findSingle,
34	      findUniqueOrThrow: mapping.findUniqueOrThrow,
35	      findFirst: mapping.findFirst,
36	      findFirstOrThrow: mapping.findFirstOrThrow,
37	      findMany: mapping.findMany,
38	      create: mapping.createOne || mapping.createSingle || mapping.create,
39	      createMany: mapping.createMany,
40	      createManyAndReturn: mapping.createManyAndReturn,
41	      delete: mapping.deleteOne || mapping.deleteSingle || mapping.delete,
42	      update: mapping.updateOne || mapping.updateSingle || mapping.update,
43	      deleteMany: mapping.deleteMany,
44	      updateMany: mapping.updateMany,
45	      updateManyAndReturn: mapping.updateManyAndReturn,
46	      upsert: mapping.upsertOne || mapping.upsertSingle || mapping.upsert,
47	      aggregate: mapping.aggregate,
48	      groupBy: mapping.groupBy,
49	      findRaw: mapping.findRaw,
50	      aggregateRaw: mapping.aggregateRaw,
51	    }))
52	
53	  return {
54	    modelOperations,
55	    otherOperations: mappings.otherOperations,
56	  }
57	}

1	import { uncapitalize } from '@prisma/client-common'
2	import * as DMMF from '@prisma/dmmf'
3	import * as ts from '@prisma/ts-builders'
4	
5	import { extArgsParam, getPayloadName } from '../utils'
6	import { GenerateContext } from './GenerateContext'
7	import { buildModelOutputProperty } from './Output'
8	
9	export function buildModelPayload(model: DMMF.Model, context: GenerateContext) {
10	  const isComposite = context.dmmf.isComposite(model.name)
11	
12	  const objects = ts.objectType()
13	  const scalars = ts.objectType()
14	  const composites = ts.objectType()
15	
16	  for (const field of model.fields) {
17	    if (field.kind === 'object') {
18	      if (context.dmmf.isComposite(field.type)) {
19	        composites.add(buildModelOutputProperty(field, context.dmmf))
20	      } else {
21	        objects.add(buildModelOutputProperty(field, context.dmmf))
22	      }
23	    } else if (field.kind === 'enum' || field.kind === 'scalar') {
24	      scalars.add(buildModelOutputProperty(field, context.dmmf))
25	    }
26	  }
27	
28	  const scalarsType = isComposite
29	    ? scalars
30	    : ts
31	        .namedType('runtime.Types.Extensions.GetPayloadResult')
32	        .addGenericArgument(scalars)
33	        .addGenericArgument(ts.namedType('ExtArgs').subKey('result').subKey(uncapitalize(model.name)))
34	
35	  const payloadTypeDeclaration = ts.typeDeclaration(
36	    getPayloadName(model.name, false),
37	    ts
38	      .objectType()
39	      .add(ts.property('name', ts.stringLiteral(model.name)))
40	      .add(ts.property('objects', objects))
41	      .add(ts.property('scalars', scalarsType))
42	      .add(ts.property('composites', composites)),
43	  )
44	
45	  if (!isComposite) {
46	    payloadTypeDeclaration.addGenericParameter(extArgsParam)
47	  }
48	
49	  return ts.moduleExport(payloadTypeDeclaration)
50	}

338	    typedSql,
339	    compilerBuild,
340	  })
341	
342	  const provider = datasources[0].provider
343	
344	  const denylistsErrors = validateDmmfAgainstDenylists(prismaClientDmmf)
345	
346	  if (denylistsErrors) {
347	    let message = `${bold(
348	      red('Error: '),
349	    )}The schema at "${schemaPath}" contains reserved keywords.\n       Rename the following items:`
350	
351	    for (const error of denylistsErrors) {
352	      message += '\n         - ' + error.message
353	    }
354	
355	    message += `\nTo learn more about how to rename models, check out https://pris.ly/d/naming-models`
356	
357	    throw new DenylistError(message)
358	  }
359	
360	  await ensureDir(outputDir)
361	
362	  await writeFileMap(outputDir, fileMap)
363	
364	  // if users use a custom output dir
365	  if (copyRuntime || generator.isCustomOutput === true) {
366	    const copiedRuntimeDir = path.join(outputDir, 'runtime')
367	    await ensureDir(copiedRuntimeDir)
368	
369	    await copyRuntimeFiles({
370	      from: runtimeSourcePath,
371	      to: copiedRuntimeDir,
372	      sourceMaps: copyRuntimeSourceMaps,
373	      runtimeName: 'client',
374	    })
375	  }
376	
377	  const schemaTargetPath = path.join(outputDir, 'schema.prisma')
378	  await fs.writeFile(schemaTargetPath, datamodel, { encoding: 'utf-8' })
379	
380	  // copy the necessary engine files needed for the wasm/driver-adapter engine
381	  if (isWasmEngineSupported(provider)) {
382	    const suffix = provider === 'postgres' ? 'postgresql' : provider
383	    const filename = `query_compiler_${compilerBuild}_bg`
384	
385	    // Despite the `!testMode` condition above, we can't assume we are
386	    // necessarily inside the bundled Prisma CLI because the `prisma-client-js`
387	    // generator has a legacy entrypoint inside `@prisma/client/generator-build`
388	    // which is still used by Studio, some e2e tests and possibly more. This means
389	    // we can only rely on what's shipped in the `@prisma/client` package here,
390	    // and we have to decode the WebAssembly binaries from base64.
391	    const wasmJsBundlePath = path.join(runtimeSourcePath, `${filename}.${suffix}.wasm-base64.js`)
392	    const wasmBase64: string = require(wasmJsBundlePath).wasm
393	
394	    await fs.writeFile(path.join(outputDir, `${filename}.wasm`), Buffer.from(wasmBase64, 'base64'))
395	    await fs.copyFile(path.join(runtimeSourcePath, `${filename}.${suffix}.js`), path.join(outputDir, `${filename}.js`))
396	    await fs.copyFile(wasmJsBundlePath, path.join(outputDir, `${filename}.wasm-base64.js`))
397	  }
398	
399	  try {
400	    // we tell our vscode extension to reload the types by modifying this file
401	    const prismaCache = paths('prisma').cache
402	    const signalsPath = path.join(prismaCache, 'last-generate')
403	    await fs.mkdir(prismaCache, { recursive: true })
404	    await fs.writeFile(signalsPath, Date.now().toString())
405	  } catch {}
406	}
407	
408	function writeFileMap(outputDir: string, fileMap: FileMap) {
409	  return Promise.all(
410	    Object.entries(fileMap).map(async ([fileName, content]) => {
411	      const absolutePath = path.join(outputDir, fileName)
412	      // The deletion of the file is necessary, so VSCode
413	      // picks up the changes.
414	      await fs.rm(absolutePath, { recursive: true, force: true })
415	      if (typeof content === 'string') {
416	        // file
417	        await fs.writeFile(absolutePath, content)
418	      } else {
419	        // subdirectory
420	        await fs.mkdir(absolutePath)
421	        await writeFileMap(absolutePath, content)
422	      }
423	    }),
424	  )
425	}
426	
427	function isWasmEngineSupported(provider: ConnectorType) {
428	  return (
429	    provider === 'postgresql' ||
430	    provider === 'postgres' ||
431	    provider === 'cockroachdb' ||
432	    provider === 'mysql' ||
433	    provider === 'sqlite' ||
434	    provider === 'sqlserver'
435	  )
436	}
437	
438	function validateDmmfAgainstDenylists(prismaClientDmmf: DMMF.Document): Error[] | null {
439	  const errorArray = [] as Error[]
440	
441	  const denylists = {
442	    // A copy of this list is also in prisma-engines. Any edit should be done in both places.
443	    // https://github.com/prisma/prisma-engines/blob/main/psl/parser-database/src/names/reserved_model_names.rs
444	    models: [
445	      // Reserved Prisma keywords
446	      'PrismaClient',
447	      'Prisma',
448	      // JavaScript keywords
449	      'async',

1	import { NonModelOperation, Operation, uncapitalize } from '@prisma/client-common'
2	import { assertNever } from '@prisma/internals'
3	import * as ts from '@prisma/ts-builders'
4	
5	import {
6	  extArgsParam,
7	  getAggregateName,
8	  getCountAggregateOutputName,
9	  getFieldRefsTypeName,
10	  getGroupByName,
11	  getModelArgName,
12	  getPayloadName,
13	} from '../utils'
14	import { GenerateContext } from './GenerateContext'
15	import { getModelActions } from './utils/getModelActions'
16	import * as tsx from './utils/type-builders'
17	
18	export function clientTypeMapDefinition(context: GenerateContext) {
19	  const typeMap = `${ts.stringify(clientTypeMapModelsDefinition(context))} & ${clientTypeMapOthersDefinition(context)}`
20	
21	  return `
22	export interface TypeMapCb<GlobalOmitOptions = {}> extends runtime.Types.Utils.Fn<{extArgs: runtime.Types.Extensions.InternalArgs }, runtime.Types.Utils.Record<string, any>> {
23	  returns: TypeMap<this['params']['extArgs'], GlobalOmitOptions>
24	}
25	
26	export type TypeMap<ExtArgs extends runtime.Types.Extensions.InternalArgs = runtime.Types.Extensions.DefaultArgs, GlobalOmitOptions = {}> = ${typeMap}`
27	}
28	
29	function clientTypeMapModelsDefinition(context: GenerateContext) {
30	  const meta = ts.objectType()
31	
32	  const modelNames = context.dmmf.datamodel.models.map((m) => m.name)
33	
34	  // `modelNames` can be empty if `generate --allow-no-models` is used.
35	  if (modelNames.length === 0) {
36	    meta.add(ts.property('modelProps', ts.neverType))
37	  } else {
38	    meta.add(ts.property('modelProps', ts.unionType(modelNames.map((name) => ts.stringLiteral(uncapitalize(name))))))
39	  }
40	
41	  const isolationLevel = context.dmmf.hasEnumInNamespace('TransactionIsolationLevel', 'prisma')
42	    ? ts.namedType('TransactionIsolationLevel')
43	    : ts.neverType
44	  meta.add(ts.property('txIsolationLevel', isolationLevel))
45	
46	  const model = ts.objectType()
47	
48	  model.addMultiple(
49	    modelNames.map((modelName) => {
50	      const entry = ts.objectType()
51	      entry.add(
52	        ts.property('payload', ts.namedType(getPayloadName(modelName)).addGenericArgument(extArgsParam.toArgument())),
53	      )
54	      entry.add(ts.property('fields', ts.namedType(`Prisma.${getFieldRefsTypeName(modelName)}`)))
55	      const actions = getModelActions(context.dmmf, modelName)
56	      const operations = ts.objectType()
57	      operations.addMultiple(
58	        actions.map((action) => {
59	          const operationType = ts.objectType()
60	          const argsType = `Prisma.${getModelArgName(modelName, action)}`
61	          operationType.add(ts.property('args', ts.namedType(argsType).addGenericArgument(extArgsParam.toArgument())))
62	          operationType.add(ts.property('result', clientTypeMapModelsResultDefinition(modelName, action)))
63	          return ts.property(action, operationType)
64	        }),
65	      )
66	      entry.add(ts.property('operations', operations))
67	      return ts.property(modelName, entry)
68	    }),
69	  )
70	
71	  return ts
72	    .objectType()
73	    .add(ts.property('globalOmitOptions', ts.objectType().add(ts.property('omit', ts.namedType('GlobalOmitOptions')))))
74	    .add(ts.property('meta', meta))
75	    .add(ts.property('model', model))
76	}
77	
78	function clientTypeMapOthersDefinition(context: GenerateContext) {
79	  const otherOperationsNames = context.dmmf.getOtherOperationNames().flatMap((name) => {
80	    const results = [`$${name}`]
81	    if (name === 'executeRaw' || name === 'queryRaw') {
82	      results.push(`$${name}Unsafe`)
83	    }
84	
85	    if (name === 'queryRaw' && context.isPreviewFeatureOn('typedSql')) {
86	      results.push(`$queryRawTyped`)
87	    }
88	
89	    return results
90	  })
91	
92	  const argsResultMap = {
93	    $executeRaw: { args: '[query: TemplateStringsArray | Sql, ...values: any[]]', result: 'any' },
94	    $queryRaw: { args: '[query: TemplateStringsArray | Sql, ...values: any[]]', result: 'any' },
95	    $executeRawUnsafe: { args: '[query: string, ...values: any[]]', result: 'any' },
96	    $queryRawUnsafe: { args: '[query: string, ...values: any[]]', result: 'any' },
97	    $runCommandRaw: { args: 'Prisma.InputJsonObject', result: 'JsonObject' },
98	    $queryRawTyped: { args: 'runtime.UnknownTypedSql', result: 'JsonObject' },
99	  } satisfies Record<NonModelOperation, { args: string; result: string }>
100

Perfect! I have enough information. Let me create a comprehensive report:

---

## Monorepo Package Structure Convention Report

Based on my analysis of the Prisma monorepo, here's the exact pattern for creating a new package:

### 1. **package.json Structure for Generator Packages**

For generator packages like `@prisma/client-generator-js` and `@prisma/client-generator-ts`:

```json
{
  "name": "@prisma/your-package-name",
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "description": "This package is intended for Prisma's internal use",
  "main": "dist/index.js",
  "module": "dist/index.mjs",
  "types": "dist/index.d.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "require": {
        "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
        "default": "./dist/index.js"
      },
      "import": {
        "types": "./dist/index.d.mts",
        "default": "./dist/index.mjs"
      }
    }
  },
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "https://github.com/prisma/prisma.git",
    "directory": "packages/your-package-name"
  },
  "license": "Apache-2.0",
  "dependencies": {
    "@prisma/client-common": "workspace:*",
    "@prisma/debug": "workspace:*",
    "@prisma/dmmf": "workspace:*",
    "@prisma/generator": "workspace:*",
    "@prisma/internals": "workspace:*"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/pluralize": "0.0.33"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "DEV=true tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "build": "tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "prepublishOnly": "pnpm run build",
    "test": "vitest run --testTimeout 30000"
  },
  "files": [
    "dist"
  ],
  "sideEffects": false
}
```

**Key Points:**
- Use workspace-relative dependencies: `"@prisma/package": "workspace:*"`
- Always export both CJS and ESM with dual format outputs (`.js` and `.mjs`)
- Include type definitions for both formats (`.d.ts` and `.d.mts`)
- Version is always `"0.0.0"` (managed by monorepo tooling)
- `prepublishOnly` ensures builds run before publishing

**For non-generator packages** (like `client-generator-registry`, `generator-helper`):
- Omit the `module` field if not needed
- Simpler exports with `.d.ts` (not `.d.mts`)
- Same patterns for scripts and dependencies

### 2. **TypeScript Configuration Patterns**

**`tsconfig.json` (in package root):**
```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.json",
  "include": ["./**/*"]
}
```

**`tsconfig.build.json` (in package root):**
```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.build.regular.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "outDir": "dist",
    "rootDir": "src"
  },
  "include": ["src"]
}
```

**Root files used:**
- `/tsconfig.json` - Base config extended by all packages
- `/tsconfig.build.regular.json` - Build-specific config (compilation only, no emission)
- `/tsconfig.build.bundle.json` - Has `paths` config mapping all packages for build resolution

### 3. **Build Helper Setup (helpers/build.ts)**

Every package must have `packages/your-package/helpers/build.ts`:

**For bundled packages** (generators):
```typescript
import { build } from '../../../helpers/compile/build'
import { adapterConfig } from '../../../helpers/compile/configs'

void build(adapterConfig)
```

**For unbundled packages** (registry):
```typescript
import { build } from '../../../helpers/compile/build'
import { unbundledConfig } from '../../../helpers/compile/configs'

void build(unbundledConfig)
```

**Build Configuration Details** (from `helpers/compile/configs.ts`):

**adapterConfig** (bundled - used by generators):
```typescript
export const adapterConfig = createAdapterConfig([
  { entry: 'src/index.ts', outfile: 'dist/index' }
])
```
Creates both CJS and ESM bundles with:
- Single entry point: `src/index.ts`
- Bundled format
- Output: `dist/index.js` (CJS) and `dist/index.mjs` (ESM)
- Type definitions emitted

**unbundledConfig** (unbundled - for registry):
```typescript
export const unbundledConfig: BuildOptions[] = [
  {
    name: 'cjs',
    format: 'cjs',
    entryPoints: ['src/**/*.ts'],
    outdir: 'dist',
    outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
    emitTypes: true,
  },
  {
    name: 'esm',
    format: 'esm',
    entryPoints: ['src/**/*.ts'],
    outdir: 'dist',
    outExtension: { '.js': '.mjs' },
    emitTypes: true,
  },
]
```
Preserves source structure with all `.ts` files compiled individually.

### 4. **Binary Entry Point (bin field)**

**Only needed for CLI packages** (not generators):

Example from `/packages/cli/package.json`:
```json
"bin": {
  "prisma": "build/index.js"
}
```

**Generator packages do NOT have bin fields** - they export classes/functions as libraries.

### 5. **CI/CD Integration in `.github/workflows/test-template.yml`**

Two locations where generator packages are tested:

**Location 1: Unit Tests Job (lines ~570-592)**
```yaml
- run: pnpm run test
  name: 'client-generator-js'
  working-directory: packages/client-generator-js

- run: pnpm run test
  name: 'client-generator-ts'
  working-directory: packages/client-generator-ts

- run: pnpm run test
  name: 'client-generator-registry'
  working-directory: packages/client-generator-registry
```

**Location 2: Integration Tests Job (lines ~1225-1241)**
```yaml
- run: pnpm run test
  if: contains(inputs.jobsToRun, '-all-')
  name: 'client-generator-js'
  working-directory: packages/client-generator-js

- run: pnpm run test
  if: contains(inputs.jobsToRun, '-all-')
  name: 'client-generator-ts'
  working-directory: packages/client-generator-ts

- run: pnpm run test
  if: contains(inputs.jobsToRun, '-all-')
  name: 'client-generator-registry'
  working-directory: packages/client-generator-registry
```

**To add a new package to CI:**
1. Add a step in both test locations (around lines 570-592 and 1225-1241)
2. Follow the exact format with `run`, `if`, `name`, and `working-directory`
3. Use `-all-` filter for standard packages (same as generators)

### 6. **Directory Structure for New Package**

```
packages/your-package-name/
├── helpers/
│   └── build.ts              # Must import adapterConfig or unbundledConfig
├── src/
│   ├── index.ts              # Main export
│   └── ...                    # Other source files
├── tests/
│   └── *.test.ts             # Vitest tests
├── package.json              # As shown above
├── tsconfig.json             # Simple extends config
├── tsconfig.build.json       # Build-specific config
└── README.md                 # Optional but recommended
```

### 7. **Key Convention Summary**

| Aspect | Convention |
|--------|-----------|
| **Entry Point** | `src/index.ts` exports main API |
| **Output Formats** | Both CJS (`.js`) and ESM (`.mjs`) |
| **Type Definitions** | Separate for each format (`.d.ts`, `.d.mts`) |
| **Build Tool** | esbuild via `helpers/compile/build.ts` |
| **Bundling** | `adapterConfig` for bundled, `unbundledConfig` for preserving structure |
| **Dependencies** | Use `workspace:*` for internal packages |
| **Exports** | Use conditional exports in `package.json` |
| **Version** | Always `"0.0.0"` |
| **License** | Apache-2.0 |
| **Test Framework** | Vitest |
| **TypeScript** | Extends `../../tsconfig.build.regular.json` |

This pattern is consistently used across all ~42 packages in the monorepo that have similar build requirements.

├── tsconfig.build.json       # Build-specific config
└── README.md                 # Optional but recommended
```

### 7. **Key Convention Summary**

| Aspect | Convention |
|--------|-----------|
| **Entry Point** | `src/index.ts` exports main API |
| **Output Formats** | Both CJS (`.js`) and ESM (`.mjs`) |
| **Type Definitions** | Separate for each format (`.d.ts`, `.d.mts`) |
| **Build Tool** | esbuild via `helpers/compile/build.ts` |
| **Bundling** | `adapterConfig` for bundled, `unbundledConfig` for preserving structure |
| **Dependencies** | Use `workspace:*` for internal packages |
| **Exports** | Use conditional exports in `package.json` |
| **Version** | Always `"0.0.0"` |
| **License** | Apache-2.0 |
| **Test Framework** | Vitest |
| **TypeScript** | Extends `../../tsconfig.build.regular.json` |

This pattern is consistently used across all ~42 packages in the monorepo that have similar build requirements.

1	import type { O } from 'ts-toolbelt'
2	
3	import { DMMFHelper } from '../dmmf'
4	import { generatedFileNameMapper, importFileNameMapper } from '../file-extensions'
5	import type { FileMap } from '../generateClient'
6	import { GenerateClientOptions } from '../generateClient'
7	import { GenericArgsInfo } from '../GenericsArgsInfo'
8	import { createBrowserFile } from './file-generators/BrowserFile'
9	import { createClassFile } from './file-generators/ClassFile'
10	import { createClientFile } from './file-generators/ClientFile'
11	import { createCommonInputTypeFiles } from './file-generators/CommonInputTypesFile'
12	import { createEnumsFile } from './file-generators/EnumsFile'
13	import { createModelFile } from './file-generators/ModelFile'
14	import { createModelsFile } from './file-generators/ModelsFile'
15	import { createPrismaNamespaceBrowserFile } from './file-generators/PrismaNamespaceBrowserFile'
16	import { createPrismaNamespaceFile } from './file-generators/PrismaNamespaceFile'
17	import { GenerateContext } from './GenerateContext'
18	
19	export type RuntimeName = 'wasm-compiler-edge' | 'client' | (string & {})
20	
21	export type TSClientOptions = O.Required<GenerateClientOptions, 'runtimeBase'> & {
22	  /** The name of the runtime bundle to use */
23	  runtimeName: RuntimeName
24	  /** When we are generating an edge-compatible client */
25	  edge: boolean
26	}
27	
28	export class TSClient {
29	  protected readonly dmmf: DMMFHelper
30	  protected readonly genericsInfo: GenericArgsInfo
31	
32	  constructor(protected readonly options: TSClientOptions) {
33	    this.dmmf = new DMMFHelper(options.dmmf)
34	    this.genericsInfo = new GenericArgsInfo(this.dmmf)
35	  }
36	
37	  generateClientFiles(): FileMap {
38	    const context = new GenerateContext({
39	      dmmf: this.dmmf,
40	      genericArgsInfo: this.genericsInfo,
41	      runtimeBase: this.options.runtimeBase,
42	      runtimeImport: `${this.options.runtimeBase}/${this.options.runtimeName}`,
43	      outputFileName: generatedFileNameMapper(this.options.generatedFileExtension),
44	      importFileName: importFileNameMapper(this.options.importFileExtension),
45	      generator: this.options.generator,
46	      provider: this.options.activeProvider,
47	    })
48	
49	    const modelNames = Object.values(context.dmmf.typeAndModelMap)
50	      .filter((model) => context.dmmf.outputTypeMap.model[model.name])
51	      .map((model) => model.name)
52	
53	    const modelsFileMap: FileMap = modelNames.reduce((acc, modelName) => {
54	      acc[context.outputFileName(modelName)] = createModelFile(context, modelName)
55	      return acc
56	    }, {})
57	
58	    return {
59	      [context.outputFileName('client')]: createClientFile(context, this.options),
60	      [context.outputFileName('browser')]: createBrowserFile(context, this.options),
61	      [context.outputFileName('enums')]: createEnumsFile(context),
62	      [context.outputFileName('commonInputTypes')]: createCommonInputTypeFiles(context),
63	      [context.outputFileName('models')]: createModelsFile(context, modelNames),
64	      models: modelsFileMap,
65	      internal: {
66	        [context.outputFileName('prismaNamespace')]: createPrismaNamespaceFile(context, this.options),
67	        [context.outputFileName('prismaNamespaceBrowser')]: createPrismaNamespaceBrowserFile(context),
68	        [context.outputFileName('class')]: createClassFile(context, this.options),
69	      },
70	    }
71	  }
72	}
73

1	import * as ts from '@prisma/ts-builders'
2	
3	import { GenerateContext } from '../GenerateContext'
4	import { Model } from '../Model'
5	
6	export function createModelFile(context: GenerateContext, modelName: string): string {
7	  const jsDocHeader = `/*
8	 * This file exports the \`${modelName}\` model and its related types.
9	 *
10	 * 🟢 You can import this file directly.
11	 */
12	`
13	
14	  const imports = [
15	    ts.moduleImport(context.runtimeImport).asNamespace('runtime').typeOnly(),
16	    ts.moduleImport(context.importFileName(`../enums`)).asNamespace('$Enums').typeOnly(),
17	    ts.moduleImport(context.importFileName(`../internal/prismaNamespace`)).asNamespace('Prisma').typeOnly(),
18	  ]
19	  const importsString = imports.map((i) => ts.stringify(i)).join('\n')
20	
21	  const model = context.dmmf.typeAndModelMap[modelName]
22	
23	  return jsDocHeader + importsString + '\n' + new Model(model, context).toTS()
24	}

1	import type { BasicBuilder } from './BasicBuilder'
2	
3	export const INDENT_SIZE = 2
4	/**
5	 * Helper class for building long multi-line formatted strings from building blocks.
6	 * Can use either plain strings or `Builder` objects, that would encapsulate formatting logic.
7	 */
8	export class Writer<ContextType = undefined> {
9	  private lines: string[] = []
10	  private currentLine = ''
11	  private currentIndent = 0
12	  private marginSymbol?: string
13	  private afterNextNewLineCallback?: () => void
14	
15	  constructor(
16	    startingIndent = 0,
17	    readonly context: ContextType,
18	  ) {
19	    this.currentIndent = startingIndent
20	  }
21	
22	  /**
23	   * Adds provided value to the current line. Does not end the line.
24	   *
25	   * @param value
26	   * @returns
27	   */
28	  write(value: string | BasicBuilder<ContextType>): this {
29	    if (typeof value === 'string') {
30	      this.currentLine += value
31	    } else {
32	      value.write(this)
33	    }
34	    return this
35	  }
36	
37	  /**
38	   * Adds several `values` to the current line, separated by `separator`. Both values and separator
39	   * can also be `Builder` instances for more advanced formatting.
40	   *
41	   * @param separator
42	   * @param values
43	   * @param writeItem allow to customize how individual item is written
44	   * @returns
45	   */
46	  writeJoined<T extends string | BasicBuilder<ContextType>>(
47	    separator: string | BasicBuilder<ContextType>,
48	    values: T[],
49	    writeItem: (item: T, writer: this) => void = (item, w) => w.write(item),
50	  ): this {
51	    const last = values.length - 1
52	    for (let i = 0; i < values.length; i++) {
53	      writeItem(values[i], this)
54	      if (i !== last) {
55	        this.write(separator)
56	      }
57	    }
58	    return this
59	  }
60	
61	  /**
62	   * Adds a string to current line, flushes current line and starts a new line.
63	   * @param line
64	   * @returns
65	   */
66	  writeLine(line: string | BasicBuilder<ContextType>): this {
67	    return this.write(line).newLine()
68	  }
69	
70	  /**
71	   * Flushes current line and starts a new line. New line starts at previously configured indentation level
72	   * @returns
73	   */
74	  newLine(): this {
75	    this.lines.push(this.indentedCurrentLine())
76	    this.currentLine = ''
77	    this.marginSymbol = undefined
78	
79	    const afterNextNewLineCallback = this.afterNextNewLineCallback
80	    this.afterNextNewLineCallback = undefined
81	    afterNextNewLineCallback?.()
82	    return this
83	  }
84	
85	  /**
86	   * Increases indentation level by 1, calls provided callback and then decreases indentation again.
87	   * Could be used for writing indented blocks of text:
88	   *
89	   * @example
90	   * ```ts
91	   * writer
92	   *   .writeLine('{')
93	   *   .withIndent(() => {
94	   *      writer.writeLine('foo: 123');
95	   *      writer.writeLine('bar: 456');
96	   *   })
97	   *   .writeLine('}')
98	   * ```
99	   * @param callback
100	   * @returns

1	# Prisma Repository – Agent Field Notes
2	
3	> **Meta note**: This is the primary agent knowledge base file. `CLAUDE.md` and `GEMINI.md` are symlinks to this file—always edit `AGENTS.md` directly. When learning something new about the codebase that would help with future tasks, update this file immediately.
4	
5	- **Repository scope**: Monorepo for Prisma ORM, CLI, client, tests, etc. Many packages use TypeScript, Rust and WebAssembly (via engines), TSX, and Jest; automation relies on `pnpm`. Expect large fixture directories and generated files.
6	- **Workspace layout**: Managed via pnpm workspaces and Turborepo (`turbo.json`). Node ^20.19 || ^22.12 || >=24.0 and pnpm >=10.15 <11 are required (see root `package.json`). Top-level scripts (`pnpm build`, `pnpm dev`, `pnpm test`) delegate into `scripts/ci/publish.ts`; package-specific commands run with `pnpm --filter @prisma/<pkg> <script>`. Turborepo caches builds; run `pnpm build` from root to build all packages in dependency order.
7	- **Key packages**: `packages/cli` (Prisma CLI entry point), `packages/migrate` (migrate/db namespace + fixtures), `packages/client` (client runtime), `packages/client-generator-ts` (new `prisma-client` generator), `packages/client-generator-js` (traditional `prisma-client-js` generator), `packages/client-generator-registry` (generator registry for managing generators), `packages/client-engine-runtime` (the core part of the new Rust binary free client based on Wasm query compiler, used by `ClientEngine` class in `packages/client`), `packages/client-common` (shared client utilities), `packages/client-runtime-utils` (utility types and singletons for Prisma Client), `packages/config` (`PrismaConfigInternal` + loader), `packages/internals` (shared CLI + engine glue), `packages/engines` (Rust binaries download wrapper), `packages/integration-tests` (matrix suites), `packages/query-plan-executor` (standalone query plan executor service for Prisma Accelerate), `packages/credentials-store` (credential storage utilities), sqlcommenter packages under `packages/sqlcommenter*`, and numerous driver adapters under `packages/adapter-*`.
8	- **Driver adapters & runtimes**: `packages/bundled-js-drivers` plus the `adapter-*` packages ship JS driver adapters: `adapter-pg`, `adapter-neon`, `adapter-libsql`, `adapter-planetscale`, `adapter-d1`, `adapter-better-sqlite3`, `adapter-mssql`, `adapter-mariadb`, `adapter-ppg` (Prisma Postgres Serverless). These are built on helpers in `driver-adapter-utils`; migrate/client fixtures exercise them, so adapter changes typically require fixture/test updates. CI tests driver adapters with flavors: `js_pg`, `js_neon`, `js_libsql`, `js_planetscale`, `js_d1`, `js_better_sqlite3`, `js_mssql`, `js_mariadb`, `js_pg_cockroachdb`.
9	- **Build & tooling**: Typescript-first repo with WASM/Rust assets (downloaded by `@prisma/engines`). Multiple `tsconfig.*` drive bundle vs runtime builds. Lint via `pnpm lint`, format via `pnpm format`. Maintenance scripts live in `scripts/` (e.g. `bump-engines.ts`, `bench.ts`, `ci/publish.ts` orchestrates build/test/publish flows). Build configuration uses esbuild via `helpers/compile/build.ts` with configs in `helpers/compile/configs.ts`. Most packages use `adapterConfig` which bundles to both CJS and ESM with type declarations.
10	- **Benchmarking**: Comprehensive performance benchmarks using Benchmark.js with CodSpeed integration. See `docs/benchmarking.md` for full documentation. Key commands:
11	  - `pnpm bench` - Run all benchmarks (outputs to `output.txt`)
12	  - `pnpm bench <pattern>` - Run benchmarks matching pattern
13	  - Benchmark locations:
14	    - End-to-end query performance: `packages/client/src/__tests__/benchmarks/query-performance/query-performance.bench.ts`
15	    - Query compilation: `packages/client/src/__tests__/benchmarks/query-performance/compilation.bench.ts`
16	    - Query interpreter/data mapper: `packages/client-engine-runtime/bench/interpreter.bench.ts`
17	    - Client generation: `packages/client/src/__tests__/benchmarks/huge-schema/`, `packages/client/src/__tests__/benchmarks/lots-of-relations/`
18	  - Benchmarks run automatically on CI via `.github/workflows/benchmark.yml`; CodSpeed tracks performance over time and alerts on >100% regression.
19	- **Testing & databases**: `TESTING.md` covers Jest/Vitest usage. Most suites run as `pnpm --filter @prisma/<pkg> test <pattern>`. DB-backed tests expect `.db.env` and Docker services from `docker/docker-compose.yml` (`docker compose up -d`). Client functional tests sit in `packages/client/tests/functional`—run them via `pnpm --filter @prisma/client test:functional` (with typechecking) or `pnpm --filter @prisma/client test:functional:code` (code only); `helpers/functional-test/run-tests.ts` documents CLI flags to target providers, drivers, etc. Client e2e suites require a fresh `pnpm build` at repo root, then `pnpm --filter @prisma/client test:e2e --verbose --runInBand`. The legacy `pnpm --filter @prisma/client test` command primarily runs the older Jest unit tests plus `tsd` type checks. Migrate CLI suites live in `packages/migrate/src/__tests__`, the CLI runs both Jest (legacy suites) and Vitest (new subcommand coverage) via `pnpm --filter prisma test`, and end-to-end coverage lives in `packages/integration-tests`.
20	
21	- **Client functional tests structure**:
22	  - Each test lives in its own folder under `packages/client/tests/functional/` (or `issues/` for regression tests).
23	  - Required files: `_matrix.ts` (test configurations), `test.ts` or `tests.ts` (test code), `prisma/_schema.ts` (schema template).
24	  - `_matrix.ts` defines provider/adapter combinations using `defineMatrix(() => [[{provider: Providers.POSTGRESQL}, ...]])`.
25	  - `prisma/_schema.ts` exports `testMatrix.setupSchema(({ provider }) => ...)` returning a Prisma schema string.
26	  - Test file uses `testMatrix.setupTestSuite(() => { test(...) }, { optOut: { from: [...], reason: '...' } })`.
27	  - Run specific adapter: `pnpm --filter @prisma/client test:functional:code --adapter js_pg <pattern>` (adapters: `js_pg`, `js_neon`, `js_libsql`, `js_planetscale`, `js_d1`, `js_better_sqlite3`, `js_mssql`, `js_mariadb`, `js_pg_cockroachdb`).
28	  - For error assertions, use `result.name === 'PrismaClientKnownRequestError'` and `result.code` (not `instanceof`).
29	  - Use `idForProvider(provider)` from `_utils/idForProvider` for portable ID field definitions.
30	
31	- **Docs & references**: `ARCHITECTURE.md` contains dependency graphs (requires GraphViz to regenerate), `docker/README.md` explains local DB setup, `docs/benchmarking.md` covers performance benchmarking, `examples/` provides sample apps, and `sandbox/` hosts debugging helpers like the DMMF explorer.
32	
33	- **Client architecture (Prisma 7)**:
34	  - `ClientEngine` in `packages/client/src/runtime/core/engines/client/` orchestrates query execution using Wasm query compiler.
35	  - Two executor implementations: `LocalExecutor` (driver adapters, direct DB) and `RemoteExecutor` (Accelerate/Data Proxy).
36	  - `QueryInterpreter` class in `packages/client-engine-runtime/src/interpreter/query-interpreter.ts` executes query plans against `SqlQueryable` (driver adapter interface).
37	  - Query flow: `PrismaClient` → `ClientEngine.request()` → query compiler → `executor.execute()` → `QueryInterpreter.run()` → driver adapter.
38	  - `ExecutePlanParams` interface in `packages/client/src/runtime/core/engines/client/Executor.ts` defines what's passed through the execution chain.
39	  - `TransactionManager` in `packages/client-engine-runtime/src/transaction-manager/transaction-manager.ts` owns interactive transaction IDs and implements nested transactions using savepoints. Savepoint SQL is provider-specific (e.g. PostgreSQL uses `ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT <name>`, MySQL/SQLite use `ROLLBACK TO <name>`, SQL Server uses `SAVE TRANSACTION <name>` / `ROLLBACK TRANSACTION <name>` and has no release statement).
40	  - `Transaction` in `packages/driver-adapter-utils` models savepoint behavior as async methods (`createSavepoint`, `rollbackToSavepoint`, optional `releaseSavepoint`) instead of returning SQL via `savepoint(action, name)`. `TransactionManager` expects adapter methods for savepoints and does not synthesize provider fallback SQL.
41	  - Fluent API `dataPath` is built in `packages/client/src/runtime/core/model/applyFluent.ts` by appending `['select', <relationName>]` on each hop; runtime unpacking in `packages/client/src/runtime/RequestHandler.ts` currently strips `'select'`/`'include'` segments before `deepGet`.
42	  - In extension context resolution, `dataPath` should be interpreted as selector/field pairs (`select|include`, relation field). Do not strip by raw string value or relation fields literally named `select`/`include` get dropped.
43	
44	- **Adding PrismaClient constructor options**:
45	  - Runtime types: `PrismaClientOptions` in `packages/client/src/runtime/getPrismaClient.ts`.
46	  - Validation: `packages/client/src/runtime/utils/validatePrismaClientOptions.ts` (add to `knownProperties` array and `validators` object).
47	  - Engine config: `EngineConfig` interface in `packages/client/src/runtime/core/engines/common/Engine.ts`.
48	  - Generated types: Update both `packages/client-generator-js/src/TSClient/PrismaClient.ts` (`buildClientOptions` method) and `packages/client-generator-ts/src/TSClient/file-generators/PrismaNamespaceFile.ts` (`buildClientOptions` function).
49	  - Use `@prisma/ts-builders` for generating TypeScript type declarations.
50	  - Current known options: `errorFormat`, `adapter`, `accelerateUrl`, `log`, `transactionOptions`, `omit`, `comments`, `__internal`.
51	
52	- **Creating new packages**:
53	  - Create directory under `packages/`, add `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, `tsconfig.build.json`, and `helpers/build.ts`.
54	  - Package is auto-discovered via `pnpm-workspace.yaml` glob `packages/*`.
55	  - For type-only packages, use `adapterConfig` from `helpers/compile/configs.ts`.
56	  - Add as dependency to consuming packages using `"workspace:*"` version.
57	  - **Important**: Add resolution path to `tsconfig.build.bundle.json` under `compilerOptions.paths` for go-to-definition to work in editors.
58	  - **Important**: If the package has tests, add it to `.github/workflows/test-template.yml`. Utility packages typically go in:
59	    - `others` job (Linux, no Docker): includes `debug`, `generator-helper`, `get-platform`, `fetch-engine`, `engines`, `instrumentation`, `instrumentation-contract`, `schema-files-loader`, `config`, `dmmf`, `generator`, `credentials-store`, `sqlcommenter-*` packages.
60	    - `client-packages` job: includes `client-common`, `client-engine-runtime`, `ts-builders`, `client-generator-js`, `client-generator-ts`, `client-generator-registry`.
61	    - `driver-adapter-unit-tests` job: includes `adapter-libsql`, `adapter-mariadb`, `adapter-d1`, `adapter-pg`, `adapter-planetscale`, `adapter-mssql`, `adapter-neon`.
62	    - `no-docker` job (Windows/macOS): mirrors the `others` and `client-packages` jobs for cross-platform testing.
63	
64	- **Prisma 7 direction**: Migration from `schema.prisma` datasource URLs / `env()` to `prisma.config.ts`. Commands, tests, and fixtures should read connection settings from `PrismaConfigInternal.datasource` (or driver adapters) rather than CLI flags or environment loading. SQLite datasource URLs now resolve relative to the config file and not to the schema.
65	- **Test helpers**: `ctx.setConfigFile('<name>')` (from `__helpers__/prismaConfig.ts`) overrides the config used for the next CLI invocation and is automatically reset after each test, so no explicit cleanup is needed. Many migrate fixtures now provide one config per schema variant (e.g. `invalid-url.config.ts` next to `prisma/invalid-url.prisma`) and tests swap them via `ctx.setConfigFile(...)`. `ctx.setDatasource`/`ctx.resetDatasource` continue to override connection URLs when needed.
66	
67	- **CLI commands**: Most commands already accept `--config` for custom config paths. Upcoming work removes `--schema` / `--url` in favour of config-based resolution. When editing CLI help text, keep examples aligned with new config-first workflow.
68	  - For isolated Studio verification, you can run `packages/cli/src/Studio.ts` directly via `pnpm exec tsx` and pass a config object that preserves `loadedFromFile`; this keeps SQLite URLs resolving relative to the config file while avoiding unrelated `packages/cli/src/bin.ts` imports.
69	  - Studio is now pre-bundled into `packages/cli/build/studio.js` and `packages/cli/build/studio.css`, served only through explicit routes in `packages/cli/src/Studio.ts` via the runtime-specific `packages/cli/src/studio-server.ts` bindings, and should keep listener-level coverage in `packages/cli/src/__tests__/studio-server.vitest.ts` because a past Node regression dropped `GET` bodies by treating them like `HEAD`.
70	  - If Enter or click does not open a cell editor in Studio, verify that the current table and column are writable before assuming a keyboard regression; views/system tables and read-only columns legitimately stay non-editable.
71	
72	- **Driver adapters datasource**:
73	  - Helper `ctx.setDatasource()` in tests overrides config.datasource for connection-specific scenarios.
74	
75	- **Testing patterns**:
76	  - Tests rely on fixtures under `packages/**/src/__tests__/fixtures`; many now contain `prisma.config.ts`.
77	  - Default Jest/Vitest runner is invoked via `pnpm --filter @prisma/<pkg> test <pattern>`; it wraps `dotenv` and expects `.db.env`.
78	    - Some packages already use Vitest, `packages/cli` uses both for different tests as it's in the process of transition, older packages still use Jest.
79	  - Functional generated clients in `packages/client/tests/functional/**/.generated` import `packages/client/runtime/client.js` directly; runtime changes in `src/runtime` may need corresponding runtime bundle updates to be exercised by functional tests.
80	  - Inline snapshots can be sensitive to formatting; prefer concise expectations unless the exact message matters.
81	
82	- **Environment loading**: Prisma 7 removes automatic `.env` loading.
83	
84	- **Driver adapter error handling**:
85	  - Database errors are mapped in each adapter's `errors.ts` (e.g., `packages/adapter-pg/src/errors.ts`).
86	  - `MappedError` type in `packages/driver-adapter-utils/src/types.ts` defines all known error kinds.
87	  - Known error kinds include: `GenericJs`, `UnsupportedNativeDataType`, `InvalidIsolationLevel`, `LengthMismatch`, `UniqueConstraintViolation`, `NullConstraintViolation`, `ForeignKeyConstraintViolation`, `DatabaseNotReachable`, `DatabaseDoesNotExist`, `DatabaseAlreadyExists`, `DatabaseAccessDenied`, `ConnectionClosed`, `TlsConnectionError`, `AuthenticationFailed`, `TransactionWriteConflict`, `TableDoesNotExist`, `ColumnNotFound`, `TooManyConnections`, `ValueOutOfRange`, `InvalidInputValue`, `MissingFullTextSearchIndex`, `SocketTimeout`, `InconsistentColumnData`, `TransactionAlreadyClosed`, and database-specific kinds (`postgres`, `mysql`, `sqlite`, `mssql`).
88	  - `convertDriverError()` in each adapter maps database-specific error codes to `MappedError` kinds.
89	  - `rethrowAsUserFacing()` in `packages/client-engine-runtime/src/user-facing-error.ts` maps `MappedError` kinds to Prisma error codes (P2xxx).
90	  - To add a new error mapping: (1) add kind to `MappedError` in driver-adapter-utils, (2) map database error code in relevant adapter(s), (3) add Prisma code mapping in `getErrorCode()` and message in `renderErrorMessage()`.
91	  - Raw queries (`$executeRaw`, `$queryRaw`) use `rethrowAsUserFacingRawError()` which always returns P2010; regular Prisma operations use `rethrowAsUserFacing()`.
92	
93	- **SQL Commenter packages**:
94	  - `@prisma/sqlcommenter`: Core types (`SqlCommenterPlugin`, `SqlCommenterContext`, `SqlCommenterQueryInfo`, `SqlCommenterTags`) for building sqlcommenter plugins.
95	  - `@prisma/sqlcommenter-query-tags`: AsyncLocalStorage-based plugin for adding ad-hoc tags via `withQueryTags()` and `withMergedQueryTags()`.
96	  - `@prisma/sqlcommenter-trace-context`: Plugin for adding W3C Trace Context `traceparent` header to queries.
97	  - `@prisma/sqlcommenter-query-insights`: Plugin for adding parameterized query shapes to comments (format: `Model.action:base64Payload`).
98	  - Plugins are registered via `PrismaClient({ comments: [plugin1(), plugin2()] })`.
99	  - E2E tests for sqlcommenter plugins live in `packages/client/tests/e2e/sqlcommenter*` directories.
100	  - `SqlCommenterQueryInfo` distinguishes `type: 'single'` (single query) vs `type: 'compacted'` (batched queries merged into one SQL statement).
101	
102	- **Codebase helpers to know**:
103	  - `@prisma/internals` exports CLI utilities: `arg`, `loadSchemaContext` (less used now).
104	  - `packages/migrate/src/__tests__/__helpers__/context.ts` sets up Jest helpers including config contributors.
105	  - `packages/config` defines `PrismaConfigInternal`; inspect when validating config assumptions.
106	  - `@prisma/ts-builders` provides a fluent API for generating TypeScript code (interfaces, types, properties with doc comments).
107	  - `@prisma/driver-adapter-utils` defines core interfaces: `SqlQuery`, `SqlQueryable`, `SqlDriverAdapter`, `SqlDriverAdapterFactory`, `SqlMigrationAwareDriverAdapterFactory`, `MappedError` for error handling, `ConnectionInfo`, `Provider` type (`'mysql' | 'postgres' | 'sqlite' | 'sqlserver'`).
108	  - `@prisma/client-engine-runtime` exports query interpreter, transaction manager, and related utilities.
109	  - `@prisma/client-common` provides shared client utilities used by both generators and runtime.
110	  - `@prisma/client-runtime-utils` provides utility types and singletons for Prisma Client.
111	
112	- **Coding conventions**:
113	  - Use **kebab-case** for new file names (e.g., `query-utils.ts`, `filter-operators.test.ts`).
114	  - **Avoid creating barrel files** (`index.ts` that re-export from other modules). Import directly from the source file (e.g., `import { foo } from './utils/query-utils'` not `import { foo } from './utils'`), unless `./utils/index.ts` file already exists (in which case use it for consistency with surrounding code).
115	  - **Avoid adding useless code comments that do not add new information for the reader**.
116	    Inline code comments can be broadly categorized as answering one of the three questions:
117	    - _What does this code do?_ — never write these because they do not and cannot convey any new information that's not obvious from the code. Not only they don't add value, they are actively harmful (getting out of sync with code, distracting human readers, wasting LLM tokens etc). Descriptions of units of code like functions, classes and methods must be limited to doc comments (and describe the contract of this unit and not the internal implementation details), never inline comments.
118	    - _Why was this code written (in this particular way or at all)?_ — this is what inline comments are for. Only use them to include relevant context, background, GitHub issues, reasons behind decisions etc.
119	    - _How does this code work?_ — these comments should be exceedingly rare and may indicate poorly written or confusing code. Prefer writing code in a way that makes such comments redundant, unless required for performance or other reasons, or when the complexity comes from outside systems or packages (in which case it's more of a "why" comment than a "how" comment anyway). For well known algorithms, prefer their names and references to papers, books or Wikipedia articles over long explanations.
120	  - Do write documentation comments, and mainly do so for exported items (although intra-module documentation may sometimes be useful as well).
121	  - The correct and official abbreviation of WebAssembly is "Wasm", not "WASM". There are instances of "WASM" in the codebase, but they are wrong and you should not repeat them. Fix the capitalization whenever you incidentally touch the corresponding lines or surrounding code for other reasons.
122	  - Prefer native JavaScript private properties and methods (`#field`) over the `private` keyword in TypeScript.
123

126	  - Keep changes ASCII unless a file already uses Unicode (docs sometimes include emojis).
127	  - For new fixtures, prefer minimal config mirroring existing ones to ensure cross-platform compatibility.
128	  - When adding features that span multiple packages, build from root (`pnpm build`) to ensure correct dependency order.
129	  - Type-only imports from workspace packages work at runtime but IDE may show errors until `pnpm install` refreshes the workspace graph.

131	  - **Update this file** (`AGENTS.md`) whenever you learn something new about the codebase that would be valuable for future tasks.
132	
133	- **Knowledge reminders**:
134	  - Your training data contains a lot of outdated information that doesn't apply to Prisma 7. Always analyze this codebase like you would analyze a project you are not familiar with, and prefer the learnings from this file and this codebase over your prior knowledge. In particular, remember:
135	    - **There's no such thing as "query engine" in Prisma**
136	    - **There are no database URLs in Prisma schema files**
